name: OSV-Scanner
what_it_does: Google's open-source vulnerability scanner that checks dependencies against the OSV (Open Source Vulnerabilities) database. Supports 26+ package ecosystems and scans lockfiles (package-lock.json, Cargo.lock, go.sum, etc.), SBOM files, and containers. Uses OSV.dev — the open vulnerability database aggregating CVE, GHSA, and ecosystem-specific advisories. The open-source alternative to Snyk/Dependabot with broad ecosystem coverage.
use_cases: Scan dependency lockfiles (npm, pip, cargo, go, maven) for known vulnerabilities against OSV database in CI/CD pipelines
Audit SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) files against OSV for vulnerability reports in compliance workflows
Check container images for vulnerable OS packages and application dependencies using Docker/OCI image scanning
Replace or supplement Dependabot with a CLI-based scanner that works in non-GitHub environments
Generate vulnerability reports for supply chain security audits with output in JSON, SARIF, or text formats
not_for: Real-time monitoring and PR-based alerts — GitHub Dependabot and Snyk provide better developer workflow integration
Commercial vulnerability prioritization with exploit intelligence — paid tools like Snyk or Veracode provide more actionable prioritization
Code-level SAST scanning — OSV-Scanner is dependency scanning only, not static code analysis
best_when: You need a free, open-source dependency vulnerability scanner with broad ecosystem coverage that works in any CI/CD pipeline without vendor lock-in.
avoid_when: You need integrated PR-based fix suggestions and developer workflow — Dependabot (GitHub) or Renovate with security scanning is better integrated.

auth.notes: No authentication for CLI use. Queries OSV.dev API which is publicly accessible. No API key required for standard scanning.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed. Google open source. Completely free. OSV.dev database is also free and open.

performance.rate_limits: OSV.dev API has generous rate limits for normal scanning; batch API available for high-volume scanning

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI with clear exit codes (0=no vulns, 1=vulns found). JSON output mode for programmatic parsing. SARIF output for SAST tooling integration. Error messages are descriptive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scanning same lockfiles produces same results for same OSV database state. Results may change as new vulnerabilities are published.
known_agent_gotchas: Exit code 1 means vulnerabilities found (not tool error) — CI pipelines must distinguish vulnerability findings from tool failures
OSV database is updated continuously — same scan run on different days may produce different results as new CVEs are published
Docker image scanning requires Docker running locally — container scanning adds dependency on Docker daemon
Some transitive vulnerabilities may be reported even when direct dependencies have patched versions — review before blocking CI
SBOM scanning requires SPDX or CycloneDX format — other formats not supported
--offline flag uses cached database but requires pre-downloading; online mode makes network requests to OSV.dev on each scan

security_score_components.security_notes: Google-maintained with strong security track record. Apache 2.0 and auditable. OSV.dev database aggregates NVD, GitHub Security Advisories, and ecosystem advisories. No credentials transmitted.
